lines.x: Act One
Spring
Use your spear to strike your shield.
Use your shield to block your spear.
Use your spear to strike your shield.
Use your shield to block... to block...
To block...
...oh my. This could go on forever, couldn't it?
What can we do? I have to think of something...
Use your spear to strike your shield...
Use your shield to block your spear...
It needs an 'and then' in there.
And then, and then... Ah, got it!
And then there is a massive explosion. That would do the trick, wouldn't it?
11:15 P.M. \ Clear
Lunar New Year's Eve, Lungmen, Forward Command Post
'District 14 reporting, all clear.'
'District 5 reporting, situation normal.'
'District 1 reporting, nothing out of the ordinary.'
'Green across the board. Good work, everyone. Stay on your toes.'
'Roger.'
'Understood.'
Yaaawn...
You're on the night shift now. How's it going?
Well, the migration is going according to plan. Movement speed normal. No anomalies.
This is the only Originium reaction we need to check out. Take a look.
...'Suspected Originium explosive device activation'? What is it? Somebody shooting off fireworks in the park?
Keep reading. There's a location.
Tung Fong Street, Number 122. It's a warehouse. Currently leased by Penguin... Oh, of course.
I can only hope we're in for another calm and peaceful year.
You said it. Let's just put in our hours and get home for chow.
That doesn't mean we get to relax. Chief Wei told us to stay frosty.
You know, I've lived in Lungmen my whole life, and every year people freak out...
I've never seen the 'Nian' before. What is it?
I've never seen it either. And you asked me the same thing last year.
The greenhorns are already convinced it's just an urban legend.
If there was some kind of fire-breathing giant, we'd spot it before it got within thirty klicks of Lungmen and the defensive cannons would solve that problem pretty quick.
Must be some great big beast, highly territorial, but unlikely to ever attack a city.
That's what they say. But everybody's still on alert. I mean, it's on Chief Wei's own orders.

Yeah, yeah. Save it. I've already written you clowns up for slurping down instant noodles in the control room. Not that I blame you. That's your New Year's banquet. Still waiting for your red envelopes full of money?
Then Chief Wei's met the Nian?
You respect the chain of command, don't you?
Of course! It's because of Chief Wei that I joined the force to begin with!
It's just... to get all hands on deck, watching out for some giant nobody's seen for decades?
Chief Wei's very thorough. She covers all her bases. You know this.
That's what they say...
S- sorry I'm late! I brought Originium test results!
Um? Where's... err... Chief Wei?
Well... Not here.
Wh- wha? But Superintendent Ch'en told me to check the control center...
We're on the roof...
Head down the stairs and make a left. That great big room with all the blinking lights? That's your control center.
Aaagh, I just ran up from there. Don't tell me I went the wrong way...
Sorry to bother you! I'll get out of your hair right away!
What was that all about? Who was she?
Must be new. Looked like a techie.
Did you see how wired up she was? It's definitely her first New Year. Takes me back to my first time...
Phew, whew, sorry. Can you point me to the control center...?
...Snowsant? What are you doing back here?
Wha? Superintendent Ch'en, you beat me here?
I think you got a little mixed up.
Ch'en, Senior Superintendent of the L.G.D.
The youngest Senior Superintendent in Lungmen history, and Snowsant's superior.
S- s- s- s- s- sorry! I got lost!
Ummmmmmm... do you think maybe I'll need to be r-r-r-reprimanded for not delivering this extremely important report on time...?
No, I don't think there's any need for that. You're new and I also bear some responsibility for not telling you the way.
Come with me.
Sure... err... Yes, ma'am!
It's already midnight, and there's still so many people around...
It's like this every year. Just a precaution.
...a precaution? Oh, are you talking about the legendary Nian?
Were you in Columbia so long you never saw the L.G.D. face down the Lunar New Year?
My grandma told me stories about the 'Nian' when I was little... But they were so scary I never thought they could be true...
Four-legged beasts, covered in flames from head to toe, awakened from their subterranean slumber by humans building their cities, they... No! No way they could be real!
Stories, huh?
Lots of people think they're just stories. Whatever helps them sleep at night.
Too bad all the damage to our city throughout history isn't just stories. The names carved on the headstones aren't just stories.
It's been quiet for many years. But we have to stay alert.
You've lived in Lungmen before, but you haven't been back for long. Now you need to brush up on everything you've missed.
S-s-s-sorry! Everyone's working so hard, I guess I should probably...
*Sigh*. Just calm down...
Nobody in this building has ever met the Nian before.
We've got a lot of greenhorns who don't believe. Some of them even question the L.G.D.'s mission. It happens. I'm used to it.
Wow... Poor L.G.D....
Go report to Chief Wei and then get some rest. I want another report first thing in the morning.
Ummmmmmm... Erm! Superintendent Ch'en! Could you tell me, um, what Chief Wei is like?
When you told me I would meet her today... I um...
Before I answer you, I need you to gather up some confidence.
It was Chief Wei herself who personally sent the request for you to return from Columbia. You need to believe in yourself, and believe you can handle your mission.
What I'm trying to say is: puff out that chest, girl!
S- sorry...
*Sigh*. That means you need to stop apologizing all the time.
Just like you're talking with me, you take all the information you've got, and calmly and coolly explain it to Chief Wei, in a nice, steady pace.
Don't overthink this.
Okay, I can do it, erm...
Chief Wei.
You've worked hard. Both of you.
Fumizuki, Chief Executive of Lungmen, Woman of Mystery.
...As yet unconfirmed rumors say that she has a dedicated husband managing internal affairs.
Now, not to denigrate your efforts, but it seems this report is a bit later than anticipated.
Oh, that would be because——
Sorry. We took some extra time to straighten out the report.
And there were some individuals consuming cup noodles in Sector 22's control center. I had to reprimand them.
It was poor time management on my part. I take full responsibility, Chief Wei.
...Yes, I understand.
Why don't you introduce this lovely young lady first?
This is our senior engineer in charge of maintaining our outer perimeter installations. Codename Snowsant. I think you know her.
Th-th-th-th-th-this is our first time meeting! P-p-p-p-please call me Snowsant!
Snowsant, newly hired intern and head of Lungmen's Frontier Defense Engineering Department.
She holds the record for fastest promotion in history. And yet she has zero confidence in herself!
Um, but, I guess Superintendent Ch'en already introduced me...
This is not actually our first meeting, little Snowsant.
And yet I see a whole new woman before me. Your time in Columbia has done much for you.
You... you remember me?
How could I forget? Your talent and character are known far and wide.
Not to mention your impressive record before you even left Lungmen.
Chief Wei has known about me for so long...? I... did I do something wrong?
I make a point to know of our promising young people, that's all.
I-I-I-I-I'm honored! But I never thought anyone would notice me, on account of I'm not so good with words...
Unfortunately, I have no time for whitewashed plagiarism and self-serving rhetoric.
Sorry to interrupt, Chief Wei. We've received a docking request from a vessel belonging to one of our strategic partners.
——Understood.
Snowsant, I will be brief. The L.G.D. is welcoming some guests shortly.
This evening will be Lungmen's finest hour.
Ah, yes!
Wow, it's gotten so late...
We could have been earlier.
Ummm... Sorry. I'll look over the handbooks some more when I get home.
Good thinking.
Don't worry! When you see me tomorrow, Superintendent Ch'en, I'll be able to draw a map of every level from memory!
...Let's not get carried away.
The point is, from now on you'll be reporting directly to Chief Wei. Don't get psyched out.
R- right!
But, um... Are you not going home, Superintendent Ch'en?
I have other things to do. We're short handed, and I know a certain crowd that needs a direct approach.
Not to mention... It hasn't been announced to the public yet, but the L.G.D. has some... leads.
Poor Superintendent Ch'en...
Never mind. I'm used to it.
It's your first New Year since you're back in Lungmen. Enjoy it.
Right! Thanks!
Lungmen Park
Cheers.
Cheers!
To you, Madame Oni, and another great year with you!
It's all thanks to Madame Oni that the gang did so well this past year!
Madame Oni! When are we going to Temple Street?
...Hey, no rush. We got lots of time——
But I'm still waiting on a guest. I know she's coming. I had a nasty vision. My eyelid twitched all morning.
Huh? A guest? Who is it?
You! When did you get here?!
Obstruction of justice, destruction of public property, trespassing, disturbing the peace, assault. You made a lot of noise last year, 'Madame Oni.'
They asked for it.
If you say so.
You're a busy woman, Ch'en Sir. That must be why you're getting all mixed up and only just now remembering things from last year. Here, let's raise a glass to Ch'en Sir.
Save it. Let's skip the games. I know what you're about to do.
But you're not going anywhere now.
That doesn't make much sense, Ch'en Sir?
We owned up to last year because we did wrong. But this time, we haven't done anything. You're still gonna lock us down?
Don't play dumb. Do I have to remind you what came of your strange little rituals last year?
The L.G.D. doesn't have the manpower to play around with you, so I'm nipping this in the bud.
You think I'm playing around?
*Deep sigh*. All right, for old time's sake, Ch'en Sir.
You guys have been battening down the hatches on this year's Nian prep so hard, I was thinking Ursus already fired a shot across our bow.
So we'll be good little boys and girls this time around. As long as you play by our rules.
...Fine.
I knew it would go down like this anyway.
With one condition... If I win, you have to tell me what's got the L.G.D. on edge.
'This year's special circumstances...' You know what I mean, Ch'en Sir?
Why would you want to know?
There's always someone who wants to know.
All right. Fine. I'll play along.
That's what I like to hear! Come on, let's get to drinking!
They're so rowdy over there.
They're like this every year. Don't mind them. The rowdy types have rowdy rituals.
Back when we were on the force, we wouldn't let them get away with stuff like this.
Of course not. We even beat back the Nian.
Do you mean the time with the subsonic Originium slug?
I mean the time with the underground legion.
You weren't a cop yet then. I was.
Is that right? I can't remember.
Hey... did you just touch the board?
Certainly not. Have you ever known me to take back a move?
No, you never take back moves. You just wait until I'm not looking to swap around some pieces.
Why I never! And I resent the accusation! Ahahaha——
Humm~♪ Hummm~♪
You're in a good mood.
It's New Year's! Why wouldn't I be?
I guess that's true...
But there's gotta be more to it?
Aren't you happy you passed the L.G.D. exam?
I thought you wanted to join up just because of your dad, but it turns out you're really into it?
There's that. It's always better to chase your own dreams than be at the mercy of others.
So unlike you...
This is the way.
But you're really so cheerful just for my sake?
*Sigh*... do you have to say it like that...?
Heh. Thanks.
Cards on the table. That's the way to go.
And here I thought you made a quick buck playing the forex market on Victoria's slide. I had you all wrong.
Hey.
Or did your investments in Rim Billiton pay off? It was a mining thing... I think I remember the transfer process wasn't exactly above board.
Y- you know a lot about this...
But of course. We're friends, aren't we? Be-a-trix?
I'm serious! You better not have been digging up dirt on me!
Why I never.
But maybe the L.G.D. would jump at the chance to get their hands on you. And wouldn't that be a great service?
Nooo way.
Anyway. There is one other bit of good news I won't keep from you.
Oh yeah. You're happy that we're out here having a nice stroll, that there's a party, and that all she can do is stare at L.G.D. data and maybe slip out on patrol for some fresh air.
You know all that too?!
Woman's intuition. How else do you think I passed that exam without studying?
Wha, no way! You didn't like bribe the L.G.D. or pull some other shady move...?
Ooh?
Wow, a firecracker! Now that's the sound of New Year's.
Huh? What's with you? Why'd you... Oh.
...What?
Are you still afraid of firecrackers at your age?
No. It just makes me uncomfortable that someone would use an uncontrollable explosive like that for such a frivolous purpose.
Explosive? A firecracker?
Yes. Firecrackers are an Originium explosive device, are they not?
Isn't that kind of blowing things out of proportion?
——Nonsense.
...Are you sure?
Yes.
Well, I'd like to relive my childhood for a bit, so why don't we go buy a couple bundles to play with——
Oh yes, I just remembered, there's a report on the investigation into you sitting in a certain drawer...
...W- we'd better get along to that party.
Attention all units: a vessel is mooring. Attention all units: a vessel is mooring.
Doctor, we've arrived.
Is this your first time seeing Lungmen at New Year's?
It's easy to get distracted by the festive atmosphere. But don't forget we have a job to do.

opts: To 'ring in the New Year,' right?

lines.x: The 'Nian' is a legend... or so they say.
But the records show that it is a real, if anomalous phenomenon.
Around the turn of each year, Lungmen, actually all of Yan, and even the parts of Ursus that wander nearby, get attacked.
The documented records of damage to each city don't match up very well, and the earlier legends are pretty murky.
Some speak of giant, man-eating monsters. Some tell of a strange army. And still others speak of a group of mysterious casters.
There's even a conspiracy theory floating around that some organized crime syndicate or other shadowy group fabricated the whole 'Nian' thing to cover up their activities...
...Speaking of, weren't you listening in the meeting, Doctor?
Welcome to Lungmen, Rhodes Island.
Chief Wei is waiting at the L.G.D. Please come with me.
The point is, we'll go see Chief Wei first. She'll explain the important details.
Say... Doctor, have you seen Lava?
H- how many bottles is this?
Madame Oni, let's not..
The number!
Seventeen!
Ugh... Since when... can you drink like this?
The L.G.D. has the occasional... get-together.
What ngaang gaau 'get-togethers' are you talking about... bullshit... ugh...
Gah, it's so hot! Hey, get some ice water, or beer or whatever!
I think you've had enough, Madame Oni...
Take care of her, and don't go anywhere.
*Sigh*, come on, Madame, have some water.
*GULP* *GULP* Ahh!
Hey, Ch'en... Next time you wanna pay a visit... *hiccup* You don't have to come up with some... crazy excuse, you know?
We live in different worlds.
Now, since you admit defeat, I'll... nnng...
Um...? Ch'en Sir, you're looking a little wobbly there.
I'm fine.
...You guys better get ready. If Chief Wei is right, then all of Lumgen is gonna hafta bland tofether...
Ch'en Sir, are you really okay?
*Deep breath* Pfft——
When she wakes up, you tell her, the L.G.D. got a lead on the 'Nian.'
A lead? Like a giant footprint or something?
Crazy huge craters, melted buildings, massive scorch marks.
Woah... I heard the Nian was actually a huge Originium slug thousands of miles long... is that true?
Who knows?
Our scouts couldn't find anything that looked like casting, but those marks definitely weren't there long.
Point is, if there's really something out there...
You guys gotta pitch in.
Why do we gotta help out the fuzz?
Because you can fight.
...Hey now, I like the sound of that. Ch'en Sir ain't all bad!
Don't you think it's really hot out today?
Early spring.
It's midnight. This is an early spring?
Whatever. Did that Lung get back to you yet?
Not yet. This isn't really what the L.G.D. messaging system is for.
Standard duty is off today, right? And it's my private channel anyway.
Heh heh. She's probably so pissed off she's moping around the alleyways cursing herself.
...Wei, is that a fire?
What are you on about?
It's just, why is it heating up even after the sun's gone down?
To tell the truth, I have a bad feeling about this.
It must be hard being apart from your beloved daughter. A man of your age, you've spoiled her rotten.
Like you would know.
Give it up. Don't change the subject. You think I'm going to let you cheat again?
...Hrm.
Shift's up.
Is your report ready?
It's been ready. If you get here first tomorrow, do me a favor and get me a coffee.
Honestly...
Oh, this is for you, to pass the time.
What is it?
...'Tales of the Nian: A Compilation of Catastrophes from Several Countries'?
Nah, what's the point. The Nian is some kind of mythological construct. How do people still believe in monsters and stuff?
What the hell is wrong with people these days? It's only been a few years and everything's totally different.
Aaahh, let's see here. Guh, I can't make sense of any of this new crap.
——Re... u... nion?
Hmm... Hmm...?
Come on? This is all we got?
Then I'll just have to use my imagination... Or not. What if they can't handle it...
Anyway, I'll figure it out.
Nnng... Mmf...
That should be good? Let's start it up and see...
——*Electrical hum*——
System activated.
Experiment log activated. Voice print recognized. Operator: Snowsant.
Current room temperature: 14℃. Analysis: room temperature abnormal.
It worked...! Whew, using all that electricity paid off... Wait, abnormal?
Hm... There was a calibration log around here somewhere... where was that?
Number of calibration logs: 1.
This one?

Room temperature analysis: significantly higher than regional average temperatures for this date. Presumed to be due to experimentation. No heat source found. No Originium reaction found. Added to log.
Hmm... The Originium detection function was a later addition. And it seems pretty sensitive...
Let's extend the range a bit.
Range extension failed. Cause: large amount of unresponsive objects in range. Presumed system failure. Troubleshooting in progress.
Let's see... The Originium reaction detector function uses feedback ripples from Originium reactions, so if an object isn't responding, that means it's undetectable to Originium Arts——
It's insulated from Originium reactions? A material totally resistant to Originium...?
...That's impossible!
Ugh... So it's actually malfunctioning... Wahh... the power bill...
And that is the scope of the task.
Rhodes Island has a history of excellence in dealing with such issues, and I look forward to our cooperation.
Thank you, Chief Wei.
Amiya, the leader of Rhodes Island. She may be young, but she is well regarded.
(Doctor! Have you found Lava yet? Still?)
Ahem. Chief Wei, um...
Can we register the operators that will be working in Lungmen without them being physically present?
...Do you speak of Miss Lava?
Miss Lava is an expert in Catastrophes, especially those of the distant past. It is thanks to her that we put our trust in Rhodes Island.
Of course, we understand that Rhodes Island has many outstanding operators. But Miss Lava's familiarity with the Nian——
Chief Wei! Forgive the interruption! It's an emergency.
Go ahead.
There are unidentified personnel in the city center.
...They have Reunion icons.
These marks...
They show they've already entered the city.
But it should be only a few of them. And among those, only one matches the records.
...Never mind.
No matter who they are, I will put a stop to them.
Never again.
Act Two
Greetings
Drop your weapons and step back.
I said now!
These guys aren't even human. No need to show mercy!
Do you copy? All targets in Sector 7 are confirmed to be 'Terracotta'!
Are they really Reunion?
It's a diversion. Confirm their identity first.
Chief Wei! The situation has changed!
There's... there's more of them. Almost every squad on patrol has spotted Reunion elements!
A- another report of a brief spike in temperature in some areas of the city. What are the monitors doing?!
No reaction! But looking at the other numbers everything does seem normal...!
The enemy attacked as soon as our squad approached. They did not respond to any hails. We are unable to establish communication.
The enemy is scattered! They're weak, resistance is low, but there's lots of them!
Is this really Reunion?
The heat source is shifting. Find a way to track it!
There are no breaches in the city's defenses, they couldn't have gotten in——
Don't panic!
Summon even-numbered formations. Worst case scenario, engage the enemy in the city. Give priority to evacuating civilians.
The downtown area is densely populated, especially Temple Street. Have all police officers within that district focus on evacuations.
Contact the satellite cities to check if there are any breaches in the defensive lines, confirm the enemy's means of ingress, and hold firm.

Reunion should be long gone. We need to identify the enemy. Getting control of the situation is our main task. This is not the first time Lungmen has suffered an abnormal occurrence at New Year's. No need to panic.
Stand together. The L.G.D. has nothing to fear.
Right!
Apologies, Amiya, Doctor. While you have only just arrived here in Lungmen, we have no time to offer you our hospitality.
Don't worry. Our agreement has been in effect since the moment Rhodes Island moored.
Please leave it to us. We'll assist the L.G.D.!
W- what's with those things?
They look familiar, but weren't they——?!
Aiee! Over here too!
Hey, get out of the way...!
Th- thanks!
Don't just stand there! Listen to the announcements and go to your designated evacuation zones!
Keep to the main streets until you find the police. Hurry!
R- right!
They don't talk. They don't bleed. It's like cutting into a sea sponge. Even their bodies disappear.
'Terracotta.' A fitting name.
Calling them 'Reunion' would cause unnecessary panic, Officer Ch'en. They are not flesh and blood people.
Understood. Urban districts secured.
Thank you for your efforts. Unfortunately, simply killing the enemy seems futile.
We've been unable to discern the true nature of the 'Terracottas,' but their strength has continued to grow over the past few hours.
It's hard to imagine at this scale, but could it be some kind of Arts?
That's what's most troubling, our Originium reaction monitors are still unresponsive. But that proves this cannot be Reunion.
...I'm absolutely sure of that.
It's a disguise, just an illusion.
But this image of Reunion strutting down the streets of Lungmen...
It's... disturbing.
They are completely insulated against Originium. We had thought this impossible.
But it's happening. How did they infiltrate Lungmen?
Our conventional methods have failed. We have no indication of how the enemy 'appeared.'
It is as if they've been here all along, standing in silent ranks. Charging, routing, and charging again.
...Just like the tide.
I'll prepare our defense in accordance with the contingency plan.
Be careful.
The L.G.D. can't afford to lose you.
Whoever their master is, they're just creating illusions. Other than causing a little chaos, is there any real threat to Lungmen?
They go down in one hit.
(These things are freaking me out.... If we don't move, they just stand there in those perfectly straight rows?)
(But they'll come rushing as soon as we pop our heads out.)
(Keep your voice down. They're very weak. Let's hurry up and find a way to get the jump on them.)
(——Wait, what are you doing?!)
Hey, what are guys doing? Get back here!
——No! You're not with us! Who are you?!
When did these things get in here?!
Wh- what's with your face under your helmet?
Why, what the hell are you?! Get away from me!
What's all the ruckus? The L.G.D. freaking out just 'cause they ran into an enemy that looks like them?
Get up!
R- right! I'm so sorry!
...Wait, you're not Ch'en Sir. Who are you to give me orders?
Just roll with it.
Where is Ch'en Sir?
She's is organizing a defensive line for the urban districts. She's going to block off the enemy and wipe them all out.
These things just popped out of nowhere. What makes her think that this would be the way to get rid of them all?
This is what Chief Wei determined.
How am I supposed to trust your judgment when you're so on edge you're slipping up left and right?
Ugh, I'm still dizzy.... Hey! One last question!
I have no obligation to answer to the general public. The L.G.D. is here to follow Chief Wei's orders.
I just wanna ask, is the L.G.D. out there rumbling with people from Ursus, or like Yan folk from Jiangqi? Maybe some other heavy drinkers I'm forgetting?
Or are you guys running around with hooch in those canteens instead of water?
Uh.... I can't really answer that, and I really don't know....
Ugh. I'm just trying to live down the shame of a certain Ch'en drinking me under the table.
Pull yourself together! Do some stretches!
We don't want the general public involved...
Gah, my head... Gotta sober up... What did you just say?
Erm. I said, well... I guess there's no need to turn down help from upstanding citizens....
We lost the heat source. Can't our instruments trace it?
No, no, no! Conventional monitoring doesn't work. It only uses temperature to determine a rough location, and with severe lag!
Receiving reports from all squads, the enemy appeared out of nowhere in the city. Still no trace of Originium Arts!
Let's rout them like we did before...
C- contact. The Terracottas are changing again, they—— they——
...What happened?
Chief Wei! They've started learning to use weapons and, more importantly, contact with the enemy reveals unique traits in each individual....
It's as if they're really living people——
Impossible! Who could make such a realistic illusion? There must be a caster! Send out a scout team to find them!
They've taken out our equipment! You still think they're fake? They're not illusions, they're real!
But..... But this.... It doesn't make any sense at all!

opts: Is there anything I can do to help?

lines.x: ......Thank you for the kind words, but you need not trouble yourself.
You've dispatched Rhodes Island's operators to assist the L.G.D. in securing the urban districts, and that is the greatest of help to us.
Is Chief Wei here——? Ahem!
I'm sorry! I didn't mean to...! You, you're....
The leader of our partner organization, Rhodes Island Pharmaceuticals. I believe we can put it that way for now. 'Doctor' is the appropriate form of address.
S-s-s-s-so sorry, Doctor! And Chief Wei, what's going on outside?
All information on the 'Terracottas' has been disseminated to all members of the L.G.D. through the terminal.
Oh, I'm sorry, I forgot there was such a thing...
It- it still isn't finding that heat source? This is very strange....
Uh oh.... Temperatures rising throughout Lungmen. We can't use thermal imaging. None of our instruments can detect the heat source itself——
We can't confirm that the 'Terracottas' are directly connected to the heat source. And it is likely that the defensive line will collapse if we reallocate manpower.
Wouldn't it be better to look at it the other way around?
What?
Like, what if we took all the Originium-powered equipment in the city and turned it to max, then look for wherever we *don't* detect an Originium reaction...?
S- sorry, I was out of line. This isn't really my wheelhouse....
No... That's just crazy enough to work! Hey, how much equipment does the L.G.D. have the authority to deploy directly?
It's a lot more if you include civilian ones.
But if we have to overload everything, all of Lungmen will be paralyzed by the time we find the heat source...
Paralyzed...? What kind of damages are we talking about?
I don't know. Tens of millions?
Maybe we'd b-b-b-better not do it...
No. Just do it.
Nian sightings have become less and less common since the development of nomadic city technology. We have not had the opportunity to study the 'Nian'.
The L.G.D. will do whatever it takes to uncover the secret of the 'Nian,' even if it's just the tip of the iceberg.
I trust Snowsant's judgment.
Thanks, but, but shouldn't we make careful projections before....
——! Sector 4's line has been breached! I can't raise the team leader!
Other lines have engaged! There are too many enemies!
All teams, keep the airwaves clear at all times. Repeat, all teams, keep the waves clear!
Dynamic codes will be sent to team leaders every ten minutes! Use these to identify yourselves!
Damn it, if it's not one thing it's another.
Stop complaining! The rest of you, form up! Get the wounded back. Don't let them break through that barricade!
Ugh, that really brings back some bad memories.
They're getting tougher. But they're no Reunion. And we beat Reunion.
Regroup!
No... You- You...
You're..... you're supposed to be dead....
That's a fake, you moron! It's not that Infected. Come on!
C- Captain!
Don't be *Lungmen profanity* stupid. They're all fakes! All of them!
I know, I know, I know! But to see them again, all of a sudden like this, I——
You again?! I just killed you a second ago!
None of you have any issues to work out with your colleagues? This is a great opportunity! Dig in your heels and give 'em hell!
This is as far back as we go. Are you going to let them into L.G.D. HQ?!
Yes!
Lungmen Guard Department
Chief Wei! We've found an anomalous area on thermal imaging.
We've got a lock on the heat source! It worked!
But we're out of time... Some of the targets have breached our defensive lines!
Re- report! We're getting distress signals from multiple teams!
Where's Officer Ch'en?
Officer Ch'en met up with Rhodes Island operator Amiya and are currently engaging the enemy approaching the L.G.D.!

opts: You sure you don't need any help?
Why don't you let me give you a hand?

lines.x: ...Chief Wei?
Lungmen trusts the Doctor's abilities, and since Rhodes Island operators have joined the fight, the Doctor should enjoy command authority equal to that of Officer Ch'en.
We'll leave it to the Doctor.
The enemy moving outside the containment zones will harm the civilian population, we have to——
Wait, we've picked up a strong Originium reaction.... a sandstorm? This, this is——
Special transmission incoming! It- it's Inspector Lin!
Hasn't Old Lin been retired for seven or eight years?! What's he saying?
He- he said he tossed all the enemies who slipped out of containment into a dumpster...
And, he still has a message for Chief Wei....
...Officer Lin is a hero of the L.G.D. and we should respect his opinion.
Yes!
Big Wei, how are the greenhorns? Is this how they're doing things? Why not make a special dispensation, throw me and Little Wei into the ring and I promise we'll have this done in under ten minutes..
........Chief Wei?
........Heh, I miss those days, when we were all young.
But the L.G.D. hasn't fallen so far that we need to rely on those two. Contact Officer Ch'en, and let us trouble the Doctor to command the Rhodes Island operators.
Time to fight back.
I told you the New Year's couplets you wrote were inauspicious. Just now I saw several faces who should not be alive.
That's my fault now? And how many times have I told you not to use those old pet names in front of the men?
Who is it who wears the pants around here?
Right. That's why *she's* Big Wei.
Whatever. So what do we do now?
Well, I never thought I'd live to see another Nian.
Reminiscing about our days on the force?
No. And my daughter isn't even home. There's no point in heading back there.
Let's see what they've got in store this time around. No matter who we've become, our duty to protect the people hasn't changed.
...Nng... I can't feel my arms... Shit...
These things, why do I feel like they're getting stronger....?
What?
What's going on? Why are these people charging us without so much as a word?
I think you've got it a bit twisted, Waai Fu. I'm afraid these aren't quite 'people,' you know?
They're more like ghosts, or something?
G- ghosts or whatever wouldn't go down this easy, would they?
Legend has it that as the New Year approaches, all kinds of monsters will come out to plague the people. Could be something like that.
Monsters can be seen and touched! Ghosts can't!
You say that, but he vanished right as you kicked him...
Huh? Where are you pulling us all of a sudden? And you're looking kind of stiff, Waai Fu.
Take heart.
You civilians should be in your designated evacuation zone. It isn't safe here!
You're hurt, Ah Sir. Your right arm is broken and you've lost a lot of blood. It's at least an hour and a half walk from here to the L.G.D. Can you handle it?
Ugh.... I'm fine....
Aak, you better not...

If I might speak freely, I come from a long line of doctors. And since childhood I have taken it upon myself to save lives and care for the wounded. Whenever I encounter some excitement on the street, I always rush to see if I can be of aid——
W- what are you doing?
——Stopping your bleeding, dressing the wound, applying anaesthetic, giving you a whole new lease on life.
That last one sounds a bit overboard.
It's under control. Come now, Ah Sir, how often do you get a shot at free health care?
I really appreciate it, but there's something off about y——OW! Hands off! Hey!
What was that drug? Are you licensed? Show me your papers, or else——
——Ah.
Heh, heh? The pain's actually gone...?
And what's that fruity smell....?
The new stuff is watermelon flavored. Congratulations on being the first to try it!
Why would you have watermelon flavored injections?
Honestly, the pain relief is just a placebo effect. Your bleeding has stopped, and you'll certainly have no trouble returning to the L.G.D. in one piece.
Once there, do remember to get formal medical treatment, as a simple debridement will not solve the problem, and there is no way to know what substance caused the wound.
........Did you just admit you're informal?
This is an emergency!
Let's get you up, Ah Sir. You're all right.
I really do feel all right...
Thank you very much, and I'll report this assistance to my superiors when the battle is over!
No, no, no, let's not go reporting things. Safe travels, Ah Sir. Take care!
...What a weirdo.
Whew! And that's my good deed for the day!
Experimental Epinephrine Reagent No. 3.... Effective..... No short-term adverse effects.... Continuous observation.... Smells of watermelon, origin unknown.... And that's that!
Origin unknown?
Tried it, it worked, it didn't kill anyone, that's what I call good medicine!
Good thing it worked, or we'd have to knock you out and hand you over to the L.G.D. to calm things down.
Those ghosts are making trouble all over the place. Is that why Mr. Lee had us out on the street all of a sudden?
I definitely don't want to give up the streets to them, but if it's g-g-g-ghosts.... What can you do? You can't punch or kick them.
But you just——
You can't fight a ghost with your fists! It can't be done! No way! I can't do it!
You were doing that literally two minutes ago...
Well, I've been short of work lately, so why not just leave them to make some patients?
Like a bicycle repairman who leaves nails out on the street.
Keep going down the path of wickedness and you'll get what's coming to you.
I'm not worried. I know far too many happy, healthy people who've earned a comeuppance. My turn is a long way off.
By the way, Hung, aren't you curious about the 'Nian'?
I just want everyone to be safe and sound.
You're! So! Dull! What about Waai Fu? Want to sneak into the containment zone and take a look?
I won't stop you, but I'll tell Mr. Lee all about it.
Don't you want to go toe to toe with a literal legend? Bam! Pow! Bang! Uppercut! I've heard the Nian is a tough, bloodthirsty demon. One you can actually see and touch!

Ah. And if you defeat the Nian, your old man, in pursuit of the ultimate truth of martial arts, would finally be willing to fight you. And then, at long last, father and daughter communicate with their fists, reconciling once and for all! What a beautiful story....
Hey, Waai Fu, I was just kidding, don't be like that.... Wait, Waai Fu, where are you going...? Oh, no! Hung! Stop her!
——Mom?
Mister.... Have you seen my mom?
Mister?
——Mmh, hot!
Huh?
Lady, where did that man go?
It's probably not a good idea to let a guy like that, silent and armed to the teeth, around kids, huh?
But Mom told me to find a policeman if I got lost...
You don't have to do everything your mom says.
But...
No buts, little one. We've got the battle of the century going on out here. You run along and play somewhere else.
Um, okay.... Thank you, lady!
What a good kid.
Hmmm...?
Isn't a sweet little show like that supposed to make you think, 'Oh, maybe she's not so bad,' and lower the dagger?
Is one woman supposed to be able to wear out the entire L.G.D.?
I'm not one to mince words, so let's get right to it. You're no ordinary warrior. What's your name?
Lava.
What's with people these days? That can't be your name. It's too weird.
It's my codename.
That's kind of a pain. What's the difference between a name and a codename anyway? Is it really so important to express your identity as an independent organism?
You're the one who asked!
Besides, there's no way you could understand us, 'Nian'.
Wow, you recognized me right away.
That's what they used to call me, but I don't think 'Nian' is cool enough. Can we change it? I want a badass codename like 'Lava.'
...You won't get away, the L.G.D. can track you.
I don't have to run. Come, Tinmen.
This is the weapon you took from the Lungmen central database.
What can you do with such weak illusions?
Oooh - you cut through the air with a dagger? I've never seen Arts like that before. Pretty badass!
Thanks for the compliment. Should I teach you?
Ho ho ho——not bad.
I didn't expect to see a massive moving metropolis. You people have some tricks up your sleeves.
And your Arts, well, I've never seen such a skillful and practical approach before.
It's a relief. I was afraid you wouldn't have made much progress.
You used the data in those records to create illusions of Reunion and the L.G.D.
Your power is not like Originium Arts, but you can't fool me. Now that I have you figured out, the monsters that have plagued Lungmen for centuries will be no more.
You're a smart cookie. Where'd you come up with this stuff?
I've been combing ancient records and modern papers in a quest to understand you.
Huh? So all the years I've been sleeping, you've been cramming trivia about me?
Pretty much.
I was born and bred to hunt your kind.
Wha? So you're like, a fan of mine?
...If wanting to kill you counts.
I never thought I'd meet someone like you.... before I forgot myself.
Oh, honey, I'm touched. I'm on the verge of tears here. It's like I'm a forgotten has-been meeting an old fan. How about it? Why don't we call a truce and go get a drink?

Since we're waiting for the Tinmen to overpower the... oh, what are they called again...? That's right! Since we're waiting for the Tinmen to beat down the L.G.D., we might as well find something to pass the time.
...According to the records, it's been decades since you last attacked Lungmen.
You have no idea what we're capable of now, and you're still so sure of yourself?
What are you capable of? You've got taller buildings, a bit more metal in your machines, and your hot pot tastes worse...
What should I be worrying about? What else can you show me?
You seem to be looking forward to it.
That I am.
You'll see. Let's call it... divination.
They're allowing divination these days? Time was, a commoner would be put to death for seeking knowledge of the heavens.
If divination's a crime, then I'm guilty as charged.
You....
I'm even more curious about you. You sure you don't want that drink?
Then make those things stop.
Is this like a 'surrender now and I'll let you live' thing?
I care more about the truth than fighting you.
The truth? What do you want to know? Seeing as we're so in tune with each other, I might consider telling you.
Your goal. And how to defeat all of you once and for all.
All of us?
Nian is only your name. The Catastrophe is far more than just you, I know that well.
What are you going to do when you know the answer? Still going to be all fighty-killy?
Yeah. Maybe. If your true form can actually be killed.
(There's a change in her attitude... A pressure in the air...)
Hey, li'l Lava.
Don't call me that. We're not friends.
Don't say that, you really surprised me, you've learned a lot more than I ever expected.
You're right, you know. At first, I wasn't planning to do anything to this city. As far as I was concerned, you were weak little folk, gradually making your way in the world.
But I have to admit, you made some real progress over the years.
And to think someone's actually been watching us all this time. That's a pleasant surprise.
You left behind horror stories and monster myths in every corner of the world. How could we not look for you?
Sometimes it was a giant, sometimes it was an army, sometimes it was Arts like a Catastrophe...
But we always grew stronger, in order to survive.
What I don't understand is how the people of ancient Yan fought with spears and halberds, while Lungmen today has huge cannons to protect the city, and in the end they all suffered the same fate?
Were you going easy back then? Or are you stronger now?
Or——
You need juuust the right amount of destruction and mayhem to make a point.
Isn't this fun? I wake up to find that the little ones who haven't changed in years are suddenly so close to the truth.
I think it's time to take off the kid gloves and bump up the difficulty.
You won't get away with it.
Ooh, fire——?
So the dagger was just an opening act. The staff is the headliner?
You people have never been willing to use your power efficiently. And that's one thing that hasn't changed.
A shield...?
I've never seen anything like it before. And it's suppressing the range of my Originium Arts, how did she do that...?
Hey! Originium Arts? That sounds pretty badass!
(But my flames just now... She used her power specifically to block them?)
(Spatial Arts are trickier.... but...)
But I do have one teensy little question. How did you come up with all this stuff while I, or I guess 'we,' were snoozing away?
No comment.
Is that right?
Let's change the subject then. A minute ago, you said my power was to use data to do up those toy soldiers.
Sorry, but that's a swing and a miss. How could I figure out something so complicated so quickly?
Turns out, you break stuff down into its simplest parts, language, Arts, anything, and it's all pretty much the same.
And the best possible material I could find was that data. Your past enemies, your fears and anxieties. It's perfect for having a little fun.
Think of it like blowing bubbles in the park.
...What are you trying to say?
When you said you had me figured out, I got this warm, tingly feeling.
I wonder if it's true.
You're...
How... The top of that building... it's melting?
Relax. All the people are gone.
...What are you going to do?
Smelt. Cast. Temper.
This city will make the perfect forge.
Li'l Lava, don't you think you'd make a good blacksmith?
Who wants to be a blacksmith?!
(Hrm, it's just like in those theses. They were so out there, I never thought they could be true. But the Nian can pull off some incredible feats without using Arts, in the traditional sense.)
(Now, just like the conclusions of those papers... I have to try this.)
Oh? The dagger slices through the air and compresses it. Is this how you use those Originium Arts?
None of the records or studies give any indication of your true form. It took long enough to determine that you were actually a communal organism rather than a single individual.
But that begs the question, why am I standing here?
Cover your ears, Nian.
Ugh.... That noise...!
'The heart of the crucible is... nothing but straw!'
Gah——That's——
Never thought I'd see you cowering.
Who the hell are you?! How do you know about that——
Ahhhh.... No... I know all too well who's behind this...
Shit. How much did Hsi tell the humans——
Surprised?
Countless researchers have spent countless years going over every record they could get their hands on, and still not one could find even a shred of hope to combat the Nian.
But there are many ancient legends in the land of Yan, and the ones that don't concern you have interesting bits to them.
Legends?
Old folk sayings like 'the din of fireworks rings in the new year' and such.
Ahhh....
So that's how it is.
I guess these things turn into legends to be passed down through the centuries, even if you don't understand their true meaning.
In a world plagued by Catastrophe, you find a way to keep things going. Isn't that nice?
I'll put a little more thought into it.... Let's build something a bit less boring.
She closed her eyes?
Wait, she's taking something out of her mouth... it's... a sword?
Slow on the uptake. And don't distract me. What if I choke?
The spatial Arts... you cut it open?
Is that so surprising? Just cut right into it. You can't do that?
*Sigh*. Never enough respect for the time-honored crafts. Always after some kind of boring old embroidered pillow or whatever.
Then let's have you feel the weight of history.
Guh!
Oh, sorry. Was that too heavy for you? Hey! Are you still alive?
So this is the power of the 'Nian,' huh? I'm not impressed.
Come on, that was pretty hard, wasn't it? Don't try to act tough here.
It's fine. I already know how to beat you. Aren't you at least a little shocked?
If nothing else because I'm actually putting up a fight?
...Ha. I think someone's getting too big for her tattered britches.
——The ground, no, the world is shaking. Does this city mean anything to you?
It's overkill, isn't it? Just to deal with you?
I'm only trying to see the best you humans have come up with. And passing the time, I guess.
How long can you keep fighting? Ten minutes? An hour? A whole day?
Until I unravel the mystery of your trickery.
You're dreaming! How can a simpleton like you perceive that which is beyond your world?
She pointed to the sky...?
'Heavenly forge, take the bounty of the earth and smelt an edge to slice the sky!'
Arise!
Act Three
Visit
Use your spear to strike your shield.
Use your shield to block your spear.
So the question is what happens when your right hand fights your left?
Apparently nothing.
Even if you win, what then?
What then indeed.
Hmmm.... But actually, wouldn't your dominant hand have an advantage?
4:21 A.M. \ Cloudy
Lungmen Outskirts
Three days since the L.G.D. lost control of District 21
Here you are, Officer Ch'en.
I can look out over the fallen city from here.
Chief Wei's decision to move the satellite cities and form a defensive line to cut off the Nian's attack was decisive.
But I never thought that one day I'd be staring down a piece of Lungmen like this.
...It's ridiculous.
That's where the Nian is.
There's a mall over there in that district I liked to go to.
And some of my friends live there.
...I'm sorry, Officer Ch'en
It's unfathomable that an entire city-state could be at a disadvantage against just one enemy.
With how long it takes to train a professional soldier versus how long it takes her to cast a warrior, it goes without saying.
Lava, Rhodes Island elite operator, master caster, and Catastrophe expert.
Her Arts transcend the shackles of space.
Lava! You're here!
Sorry, I shouldn't have engaged her like that.
Tell that to the operators we lost. The price to extract you was high.
What we need is your knowledge and skill. We don't need you killing morale with apologies.
Morale? Relying on morale in the face of an unknowable enemy will lead only to death and misery.
To defeat her and take back the city, you must confront the mystery that has plagued us throughout history.
'Her weakness.'
I thought that was your mission.
The good news is that the 'Nian' is eager to communicate with us.
The bad news is that her personality is terrible.
I had high expectations of you, so you better not tell me you're empty-handed.
Of course not.
I've managed to glean a few leads from the extensive historiography, but following up on them means more bloodshed.
You don't have to go it alone. The Nian has a nasty temperament. If anything were to happen to you...
My blood alone is enough.
Ch'en Sir, emergency communication.
We've got a bead on the Nian. She's approaching the front line.
...Tell the teams not to engage. And warn the civilians not to get involved. This isn't a game.
But she didn't bring many troops with her....
To her, numbers mean nothing. We can only slow her down.
You said she has a weakness. Out with it already. We're short on time.
Her weakness is firecrackers.
...Firecrackers?
No record of the 'Nian' has ever described her face, let alone her weaknesses. But a trip to Yan led me to some new discoveries.
Any culture with a long history will have its legends and folklore. Although some of it may have origins in more recent times, obscuring the truth.
Some theories suggest that firecrackers were the forerunner of modern Originium explosives technology, originating in some devotional folk practices.
That's a pretty common custom. You throw out the firecrackers to scare off the evil spirits and wish for a peaceful new year.
And what are the evil spirits in these ancient legends?
Wait, the L.G.D. can bring out 100,000 times the explosive power of a firecracker. This doesn't make any sense.
'Fear the noise, fear the flame.'
If the Nian's function really is 'smelting', then a firecracker doesn't pose a threat to her at all.
She... called herself a craftsman. Flame and noise are part of her work, or at least part of some kind of image she projects.
Some kind of image?
It's a theory I have to explain her utterly irrational ability to do these things without the use of Originium Arts.
So she's afraid of herself? You keep making less and less sense...
I thought it was just a legend.... But it's worth a try.
She did flinch when I used some big, noisy flame Arts.
...It could work. It's not like we have the time to apply rigorous academic peer review anyway.
We have to try something, at least. She's already breathing down our necks.
Let me say one last thing.
She's strong. There may be a way to destroy her, but the price will be high.
——The L.G.D. will make sure it isn't *too* high.
Try to avoid taking her head on. She doesn't like to kill, at least.
Second, using her power doesn't wear her out. If we get into a war of attrition with her, we're the ones who go down.
Third, for some reason, she has an interest in modern consumer goods.
...How does this help us fight her?
The hunter traps the beast by taking advantage of its nature. And what we call 'personality' is just nature in a fancy package.
Finally, beware her sword and shield. They are of utterly incomprehensible construction.
Sword and shield, got it.
I'll be the one striking at her weak point. I hope Amiya and the L.G.D. can create some space for me to do that.
She'll pay for using those Reunion illusions to toy with our Lungmen.
But command of the L.G.D....
Chief Wei and the Doctor have it under control.
Then I'll go with you.
Amiya...
It's okay, I can take care of myself.
With Rhodes Island and the L.G.D. fighting the good fight, it's time for me to do what I can...
And that's slowing the Nian's advance. No problem!
Amiya, let's go.
Coming!
These are some pretty high walls.
Hey you, the soldiers up there, can you hear me...?
Are they ignoring me? Is that any way to treat a lady?
Hmmm.... It's got a smooth surface. I'll give it a knock.... Some kind of alloy, right? Seems stronger than the brick and mortar ones?
I wonder if it'll leave a dent in my sword. I'd hate to ruin it...
Whatever. I already took it out of the sheath. No going back now.
She- she sliced through the outpost wall?
If that operator hadn't told us to look out for her 'sword,' I never would have called that a 'sword.'
Come on, I gave you a three day break and this is the best you can do?
Li'l Lava got me all pumped for you guys. What the hell?
No more tricks today?
You're out here all alone. Don't think you'll have it so easy.
I'm more than a match for you all by my sweet little lonesome...
Ooh no. I think here is where I say 'I won't soil my own hands here. You're not worth my time.'
Thanks for the reminder. That's how this was supposed to go!
Officer Ch'en, we're surrounded!
There's a lot of them, but they go down easy.
How——
Ah, right, forgot to mention.
The past three days, with nothing else to do, I've been polishing up these 'Reunion' things.
You know, the real ones weren't so different. They were expendable to you, same as my Tinmen.
I'm not sure what the whole deal is with this Oripathy thingy, but I guess it explains why forging these things feels so weird.
It makes a difference.
Officer Ch'en! Look out!
Pfft!
No way.... Did those illusions just use Arts?
If I felt like it, I could melt down that whole district and turn it into a giant Tinman. But do I feel like it? Hmm...
Nah, that sounds like a pain in the ass.
But it's not that hard to make a few Infected puppets. It's just one more process.
You..... That's not something you can joke about....
Treating the Infected like that... is too cruel!
Like what? Look, I really don't care who's Infected or whatever. Do you guys think that much about Originium slugs?
Fire!
Hm. That was kind of a wimpy bolt.
Here's what I'm going to do: I'm gonna walk forward, one step at a time, right up to your base, and destroy it.
And you can try to stop me.
Snowsant.
Aaaahhh! Sorry! I didn't mean to...
Wait, what did I do....?
Y- you're from Rhodes Island, aren't you?
Operator Lava.
I need a blueprint of the infrastructure district. The underground part.
Uh, that's classified by the engineering department, and the procedures are complicated.... What do you need it for?
I'm making one hell of a firecracker.
F- firecracker?
I'm putting every second of progress we've made since the dawn of time up against the Nian.
N-N-N-N-Nian——? Is it really a g-giant? You need explosives? Orrrr... the city's defensive cannons?
No. Before we start talking about the firing angle on those bulky things.... You can't fire a huge cannon at your own city....
...No, wait.... The cannons....
The defensive cannons....
Lava?
Those cannons... they're pretty much like a two-step firecracker, aren't they?
No! They literally couldn't be more different!
They're both based on an industrial Originium process.... they both explode.... in mid-air....
It's a two-step firecracker!
Why does it have to be a two-step?
So maybe.... But it would cause some damage to the main city....?
L- Lava? What you're muttering there is kind of the understatement of the century, but you're not really thinking that, are you?
——It's fine. I'll take care of it. Trust me.
Th- then I'll go submit the application...
That will take too long. I will add a supplementary report later.
We can't let these regulations slow us down when we're fighting for our lives.
Thanks.
B- but it takes time to sort out the documents from each department and print them out...
Ah, I know!
I can draw a simple diagram for you.
What?
Hold on, I need to find something to draw with...
...Chief Wei, is Lungmen's superstructure really that simple?
It's very complicated, of course. The power supply to the surface plane alone, not to mention the Originium engine is a tremendous undertaking.
But if Snowsant thinks she can draw it, perhaps she can.
I wanted to study up for my new job, so I went ahead and memorized every blueprint I could find!
D- don't worry! It's just about memorizing something and drawing it out. Even I can handle that!
...That's all, huh?
So many Reunion.... There's no end to them....
You go on ahead and take up a position at the next intersection.
At least pin down those Terracottas.
On it!
What about me, huh? Isn't anyone going to pin me down?
All I had to do was ask? Now that's what I call service.
(There's something not quite right about her shield——)
I'm coming, Officer Ch'en——!
Hey now, you've got a fine looking weapon at your side there. But instead you're using that hunk of junk? I'm kind of offended.
And you, little one, you seem to have some power you can't quite control. Why don't you put on a show for me?
Don't let her get to you!
I won't!
What gives? Why is everyone acting all cool these days? Don't tell me modern human society is all about repressing your feelings?
It's no fun fighting people who can't use their full power. You keep looking at my sword and shield? Go on. Take your best shot.
——Don't mind if I do!
——! You broke her shield!
So you did! Congrats.
Oh, would you look at that. Got a new one.
Pfft. I knew it...
Ch'en Sir, the enemy is hitting our defensive line, we can't hold on much longer!
...Withdraw the L.G.D., close the gate, and activate the defenses.
Stall for as long as possible.
I hope Lava has a plan.
What the hell, you're running away already?
Looks like we're almost to the end of the line here... is it that building?
It's big.
Who needs a building that tall anyway?
I'm back. Is the evacuation complete?
Other departments already left L.G.D. HQ and our teams have surrounded the area as planned.
Don't let anything through our net. I want minimal casualties.
Understood.
I'm sorry, where's Lava?
Operator Lava left after obtaining a structural diagram from Snowsant...
We have to trust her.
You mean we have no choice but to trust her.
The Nian broke through the perimeter! The gate has been breached!
Is the surveillance system still working? Track her movements!
Don't get into a firefight. It'll only end poorly for us.
Lava was right. We can't fight the Nian until we know her true form, and her true power——
And how to defeat her once and for all.
Lava!
I hope you've found a solution.
We have to blow the core of the city's defensive cannons. I'll lead her there.
And how are you going to do that? It's not like you can just ask her.
